method_atom: A bulk/boundary correspondence relating a higher-dimensional geometric/dynamical theory defined in an interior region to a lower-dimensional field theory living on its boundary, in which distinguished non-normalizable states (heavy operators) of the boundary theory are identified with localized point-like defects — curvature/conical singularities — moving in the interior. The identification is validated by matching the semiclassical two-point correlation function computed on the boundary against a suitably regularized on-shell action of the interior theory evaluated on the corresponding singular configuration.
object_atom: Three-dimensional gravity with a negative cosmological constant (Anti-de Sitter space) containing massive point particles that source conical singularities in the bulk, and the two-dimensional Liouville field theory on its boundary whose vertex operators encode those particles.
brief_reason: The transferable machinery is the holographic bulk/boundary map from boundary operators to interior point-defects plus the correlator-vs-regularized-action consistency check; what is domain-specific is the AdS-3-gravity/point-particle/Liouville realization.
